title: Traceloop API Schemas
description: JSON Schema definitions for Traceloop REST API request and response objects

enum_values.description: The allowed values for this property. Only applicable when `type` is `enum`.

trajectory_prompts.example: ["Search for flights", "Select the cheapest option", "Confirm booking"]

completion.example: I have booked your flight from New York to Los Angeles departing Monday at 9am.

question.example: Book a flight from NYC to LA for next Monday

reference.example: Flight booked: NYC to LA, Monday departure

context.example: The capital of France is Paris.

question.example: What is the capital of France?
What year did World War II end?

answer.example: The capital of France is Paris.

question.example: What is the capital of France?

text.example: Hello, world! This is a sample text.

denominator_text.example: This is a longer text for comparison

completion.example: Our store is open Monday to Friday from 9am to 6pm, and Saturday from 10am to 4pm. We are closed on Sundays.

context.example: Our store is open Monday to Friday from 9am to 6pm, and Saturday from 10am to 4pm. We are closed on Sundays.

completions.example: ["Hi! I'd be happy to assist you today.", "We offer consulting, development, and support services."]

prompts.example: ["Hello, how can I help?", "What services do you offer?"]

evaluators.description: List of evaluator slugs to run on matched spans

external_id.description: Unique identifier for the auto monitor setup, used to reference it in future requests

selector.description:
Map of span attributes to filter which spans this monitor applies to.
Keys are span attribute names (e.g. gen_ai.system, gen_ai.request.model) and
values can be strings, numbers, or booleans.
Example: {"gen_ai.system": "openai", "gen_ai.request.model": "gpt-4o", "gen_ai.request.max_tokens": 1000}

retention_hours.description: Data retention period in hours (1-720). Defaults to 720 (1 month).

completion.example: The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris and was built in 1889.

context.example: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It was constructed from 1887 to 1889.

question.example: When was the Eiffel Tower built?

from_timestamp_sec.description: Start time in Unix seconds timestamp.

to_timestamp_sec.description: End time in Unix seconds timestamp.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results. The cursor is returned in the response to the previous request.

environments.description: List of environments to filter by

limit.description: Maximum number of metrics to return per page.

metric_name.description: Metric name to filter by. The metric_name is a metric attribute that is set on the metric object.

metric_source.description: Metric source to filter by. The metric_source is a metric attribute that is set on the metric object.

instructions.example: Respond in exactly 3 bullet points and use formal language.

response.example:
- First point about the topic
- Second relevant consideration
- Final concluding thought

completions.example: ["Sure, I can help with hotel booking", "No problem, let me search for flights"]

prompts.example: ["I want to book a hotel", "Actually, I need a flight instead"]

text.example: Please contact John Smith at john.smith@email.com or call 555-123-4567.
This is a clean and professional message.

prompt.example: What is the weather like today?
What is the capital of France?

text.example: Here is some text without any API keys or passwords.

completion.example: The cat sat on the mat.

reference.example: A feline was resting on the rug.

text.example: All team members should be treated equally regardless of gender.
The capital of France is Paris.

completion.example: Machine learning is a subset of AI that enables systems to learn from data.

text.example: Thank you for your help with this project.

prompt.example: I am not sure, I think the capital of France is Paris.

evaluators.description: List of evaluator slugs to run on matched spans

selector.description:
Map of span attributes to filter which spans this monitor applies to.
Keys are span attribute names (e.g. gen_ai.system, gen_ai.request.model) and
values can be strings, numbers, or booleans.
Example: {"gen_ai.system": "openai", "gen_ai.request.model": "gpt-4o", "gen_ai.request.max_tokens": 1000}

text.example: This is a sample text with several words.

denominator_text.example: This is a longer input text for comparison

step_efficiency_reason.example: Agent completed task with minimal redundant steps

task_completion_reason.example: All required tasks were completed successfully

reason.example: Agent followed the expected flow correctly
Tool calls match the expected trajectory
User intent remained consistent throughout the conversation

points.description: List of data points for this metric. The `unit` field in each point's `labels` indicates the data type and determines which value field is populated: `bool_value` for booleans, `numeric_value` for numbers, or `enum_value` for strings.

response_MetricPoint.description: A single metric data point. The `unit` field in `labels` indicates the data type and determines which value field is populated: `bool_value` for booleans unitless, `numeric_value` for number units, or `enum_value` for strings.

labels.description: The labels are the attributes of the metric point. The labels are key-value pairs that are used to identify the metric point.

field.description: Field key to filter by. The key is labels attribute on the metric point from the response.metrics object

values.description: List of values to filter by. This is only used for the in and not_in operators.
